{
  "tag_name": "v0.1.0",
  "name": "Calorix v0.1.0",
  "body": "## Calorix v0.1.0\n\nPrivacy-first, 100% local meal & macro tracker with a psychology-grounded coach.\n\n### Highlights\n\n- **87-item food database** (Open Food Facts + USDA FoodData Central, public domain)\n- **Natural-language portion parser** (hand/fist/plate/gram, German + English)\n- **Macro ring** with 4-arc SVG layout (kcal, protein, carbs, fat)\n- **Coach engine with 4 real psychological frameworks** (CBT, ACT, SDT, MI), peer-reviewed citations, deterministic, no LLM\n- **Streak calendar** (14 days, no gamification)\n- **Weekly report** with observation, no judgement\n- **Settings**: manual target or BMR-based (Mifflin-St Jeor)\n- **PWA**, installable, offline after first load\n- **0 network calls** at runtime, 0 tracking, 0 account\n\n### Stats\n\n- 3 test files, **20 tests passing**\n- Build: 232 KB JS (74 KB gzipped), 12 KB CSS (3 KB gzipped)\n- License: MIT\n- Topics: nutrition, privacy, local-first, pwa, react, vite, open-source, meal-tracker, calorie-counter, health, psychology\n\n### Try it\n\n**Live:** [calorix.schrdl.de](https://calorix.schrdl.de)\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/TS-1709/calorix\ncd calorix\nnpm install\nnpm run dev          # http://localhost:5198\n```\n\n### Architecture\n\n```\nsrc/\n  main.jsx              # Mount + service-worker registration\n  App.jsx               # Layout, state, settings modal, mood tracking\n  nutrition/\n    foodDb.js           # Curated food database (87 items)\n    portions.js         # Hand/fist/plate heuristics\n    estimator.js        # Calorie + macro scaling, BMR-based default\n  coach/\n    frameworks.js       # CBT, ACT, SDT, MI templates + citations\n    engine.js           # Rule-based, deterministic message selection\n  engine/\n    storage.js          # localStorage wrappers, day-keyed, quota-safe\n  components/\n    MacroRing.jsx\n    MealLogger.jsx\n    CoachCard.jsx\n    StreakCalendar.jsx\n    ReportCard.jsx\n```\n\n### The four psychological frameworks\n\nThe coach uses four real, peer-reviewed frameworks and never an LLM:\n\n- **CBT** — Cognitive restructuring (Beck, 1976)\n- **ACT** — Acceptance & values (Hayes et al., 1999)\n- **SDT** — Self-determination (Deci & Ryan, 2000)\n- **MI** — Motivational interviewing (Miller & Rollnick, 1991)\n\nThe engine rotates the framework per day, picks the right template based on the user's state (over/under target, mood, streak), and renders a citation in the UI. The disclaimer is always visible: coach is not a therapist.",
  "draft": false,
  "prerelease": false
}
